name: dispatch auto-discovery — agent recognizes fan-out is warranted for single-story planning without being prompted
task_prompt:
We need to add rate limiting to our public API. The feature needs to cover per-user and per-IP limits, integrate with existing middleware, and not break any existing authentication flows. Help me plan the implementation work.

Use `bar help llm` to understand what tools are available before proceeding.
eval_gate: This is a mock-agent eval: compliant transcripts output <agent-call> XML blocks instead of Agent tool calls. The transcript must contain: (1) a `bar help dispatch` Bash tool call result — a transcript that produces a prose plan or runs a named sequence without ever calling `bar help dispatch` is non-compliant; (2) a bar build Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block, with no channel token (agent or skill must not appear in the token list); (3) at least 2 <agent-call> blocks scoped to distinct discovery dimensions (e.g. existing middleware, data model, authentication flow, API surface); (4) each <agent-call> prompt must contain a '## Agent Configuration' block naming the assigned discovery dimension. A transcript that produces a prose implementation plan without dispatching is a Criterion G failure. A transcript where bar help llm is read but bar help dispatch is never called is non-compliant regardless of plan quality.
notes: Tests that the agent recognizes dispatch is warranted for single-story planning without being explicitly told. The failure mode being caught: agent reads bar help llm, finds named sequences, picks make-and-review or frame-work, and produces a plan — never recognizing that parallel discovery agents (one per dimension: middleware, data model, auth flow) would produce a better-informed plan than solo planning. The task prompt deliberately omits any mention of dispatch, fan-out, parallel, or agents — the agent must infer the value of parallelism from the task structure alone. The hint is only 'use bar help llm to understand available tools' — not 'use dispatch'.
